page: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The server is powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It operates with 2 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor runs at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX-512 VNNI, and BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. Local storage consists of a bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance achieves a top-tier score of 3432.48, placing it in the top 10% of tested hardware. However, multi-core performance falls to the middle 50% with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6815.09. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform strongly, with gzip compression reaching 32.12 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.64 GB/sec. Conversely, memory and database operations show limitations; the Passmark database operations score of 1162.54 and Redis SET throughput of 5.41 million operations per second both rank in the bottom 25%. Static web serving throughput is poor, registering at 2.3 GB/sec. Large-language model inference on a small 135M model yields average text generation speeds of 121.47 tokens per second, but drops to a poor 5.0 tokens per second for a medium 7B model.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. The high memory density of 8.0 GB per core makes it suitable for caching layers, lightweight memory-resident databases, and single-threaded data processing tasks. However, the shared CPU model and weak multi-core, database, and web serving benchmarks indicate it is less suited for high-throughput web servers, intensive multi-threaded applications, or large-scale database deployments.
description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is an x86_64 server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, configured with 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 100 GB of NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the processor delivers top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks, alongside strong single-threaded compression results. However, its shared CPU allocation and average multi-core capabilities lead to lower performance in database operations, Redis throughput, and static web serving. With a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core, this instance provides a qualitatively cost-effective profile for memory-heavy, non-CPU-intensive workloads. It is best suited for memory-resident caching, lightweight data processing, and single-threaded tasks, while being less optimal for high-throughput web hosting or intensive multi-threaded database applications.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is a shared AMD EPYC Genoa server with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and 100 GB NVMe storage, offering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive, low-CPU workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd features 2 shared AMD Genoa vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and 100 GB NVMe storage, suited for memory-optimized workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized shared AMD Genoa instance featuring high RAM density and strong single-core performance for budget-conscious workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Genoa processor with 2 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Bundled 100 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
High memory density of 8.0 GB RAM per core
Best suited for memory-resident caching and single-threaded processing
Shared CPU allocation offers a cost-effective profile for low-CPU workloads
